annotations.label: Chart on a topological space

anchor.snippet: A chart ( U , φ ) on M consists of an open subset U of M , and a homeomorphism φ from U to an open subset of some Euclidean space

annotations.note: Charts are formalized as members of `atlas H M` of `OpenPartialHomeomorph M H` in `ChartedSpace`, generalized to any model space H rather than just Euclidean.

annotations.label: Differentiable atlas

anchor.snippet: This is resolved by the introduction of a "differentiable atlas" of charts, which specifies a collection of charts on M for which the transition maps

annotations.note: The notion of a differentiable atlas is captured by `HasGroupoid M (contDiffGroupoid n I)`, where transition maps lie in the C^n groupoid.

annotations.label: Differentiably compatible chart

anchor.snippet: one says that a chart is differentiably compatible with the atlas, or differentiable relative to the given atlas, if the inclusion of the chart into the collection of charts comprising the given differentiable atlas results in a differentiable atlas

annotations.note: Compatibility is membership in `G.maximalAtlas M`, defined as charts whose transitions with every atlas member lie in the structure groupoid G.

annotations.label: Maximal differentiable atlas

anchor.snippet: A differentiable atlas determines a maximal differentiable atlas , consisting of all charts which are differentiably compatible with the given atlas

annotations.note: `StructureGroupoid.maximalAtlas G M` collects all charts compatible with the atlas under groupoid G; for `G = contDiffGroupoid n I` this is the maximal C^n atlas.

annotations.label: Smooth structure / complex structure

anchor.snippet: A maximal smooth atlas is also known as a smooth structure ; a maximal holomorphic atlas is also known as a complex structure

annotations.note: Mathlib's `IsManifold I ∞ M` is the smooth-structure typeclass and `Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.Complex` exists, but there is no named `SmoothStructure`/`ComplexStructure` term separate from the existing atlas.

annotations.label: Equivalent differentiable atlases

anchor.snippet: is to consider equivalence classes of differentiable atlases, in which two differentiable atlases are considered equivalent if every chart of one atlas is differentiably compatible with the other atlas

annotations.note: Mathlib bypasses equivalence classes by always working with `maximalAtlas`; the equivalence relation between two atlases is not separately named.

annotations.label: Dimension well-defined on connected components

anchor.snippet: each connected component of a topological space which has a differentiable atlas has a well-defined dimension

annotations.note: No theorem establishing that the dimension of the model space is locally constant or well-defined on connected components of a charted space was found.

annotations.label: Differentiable manifold

anchor.snippet: A differentiable manifold is a Hausdorff and second countable topological space M , together with a maximal differentiable atlas on M

annotations.note: `IsManifold I n M` formalizes a C^n manifold via `HasGroupoid M (contDiffGroupoid n I)`; Hausdorff/second-countable are not built in but added as separate typeclasses when needed.

annotations.label: C^0 manifold equals topological manifold

anchor.snippet: The notion of a C 0 manifold is identical to that of a topological manifold

annotations.note: `contDiffGroupoid_zero_eq` proves `contDiffGroupoid 0 I = continuousGroupoid H`, identifying the C^0 manifold structure with a topological-manifold structure (a `ChartedSpace`).

annotations.label: Patching Euclidean pieces

anchor.section: Patching together Euclidean pieces to form a manifold
anchor.snippet: One can reverse-engineer the above definitions to obtain one perspective on the construction of manifolds

annotations.note: `ChartedSpaceCore` provides a construction of a charted space from local data (open pieces and transition maps), which is the formal version of this patching construction.

annotations.label: Differentiable function on a manifold

anchor.snippet: A real valued function f on an n -dimensional differentiable manifold M is called differentiable at a point p ∈ M if it is differentiable in any coordinate chart defined around p

annotations.note: `MDifferentiableAt I I' f x` defines differentiability of `f` at `x` via expression in charts; `ContMDiffAt` provides the C^k variant.

anchors.snippet: A real valued function f on an n -dimensional differentiable manifold M is called differentiable at a point p ∈ M if it is differentiable in any coordinate chart defined around p

annotations.label: Directional derivative along a curve

anchor.snippet: the directional derivative of f at a point p in M is defined as follows. Suppose that γ( t ) is a curve in M with γ (0) = p , which is differentiable in the sense that its composition with any chart is a differentiable curve

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `mfderiv` (the differential) directly and uses it to differentiate along curves via composition, rather than defining the directional derivative through curves as primary.

annotations.label: Directional derivative depends only on tangent vector

anchor.snippet: by the chain rule, f has the same directional derivative at p along γ 1 as along γ 2 . This means that the directional derivative depends only on the tangent vector of the curve at p

annotations.note: The chain rule for `mfderiv` implicitly gives this, but the specific statement that `(f∘γ)'(0)` depends only on `mfderiv γ 0 1` is not stated as a named lemma.

annotations.label: Tangent vector and tangent space

anchor.snippet: A tangent vector at p ∈ M is an equivalence class of differentiable curves γ with γ (0) = p , modulo the equivalence relation of first-order contact between the curves

annotations.note: `TangentSpace I x` is defined as a type synonym for the model space E rather than as equivalence classes of curves, but it serves as the tangent space at x.

annotations.label: Differential of a function at a point

anchor.snippet: This linear functional is often denoted by df ( p ) and is called the differential of f at p

annotations.note: `mfderiv I I' f x : TangentSpace I x →L[𝕜] TangentSpace I' (f x)` is the differential `df(p)` of f at p between manifolds.

anchors.snippet: This linear functional is often denoted by df ( p ) and is called the differential of f at p

annotations.label: Tangent vector via local coordinates

anchor.section: Definition of tangent space and differentiation in local coordinates
anchor.snippet: A "tangent vector at

annotations.note: `TangentSpace I x := E` (the model space) is precisely the local-coordinates implementation, with chart change-of-coordinates handled by the tangent bundle's transition functions.

anchors.section: Definition of tangent space and differentiation in local coordinates

annotations.label: Partition of unity subordinate to a cover

anchor.snippet: a partition of unity subordinate to the cover { U α } is a collection of real-valued C k functions φ i on M satisfying the following conditions

annotations.note: `SmoothPartitionOfUnity ι I M s` together with `IsSubordinate` formalizes smooth partitions of unity subordinate to a family of sets.

annotations.label: Existence of C^k partitions of unity

anchor.snippet: Every open covering of a C k manifold M has a C k partition of unity

annotations.note: `SmoothPartitionOfUnity.exists_isSubordinate` produces a smooth partition of unity subordinate to any open cover (for sigma-compact T2 manifolds).

annotations.label: C^k map between manifolds

anchor.snippet: We define " f is C k ( M , N ) " to mean that all such compositions of f with charts are

annotations.note: `ContMDiff I I' n f` defines C^n maps between manifolds via composition with charts.

annotations.label: Tangent bundle

anchor.snippet: The collection of tangent spaces at all points can in turn be made into a manifold, the tangent bundle , whose dimension is

annotations.note: `TangentBundle I M` is defined as a `Bundle.TotalSpace` and given a vector-bundle (and manifold) structure via `VectorBundleCore` indexed by the charts of M.

annotations.label: Cotangent space and cotangent bundle

anchor.snippet: The cotangent space at a point is the dual of the tangent space at that point and the elements are referred to as cotangent vectors; the cotangent bundle is the collection of all cotangent vectors

annotations.note: No `CotangentSpace`/`CotangentBundle` declaration was found in `Mathlib/Geometry/Manifold`.

annotations.label: Exact differential

anchor.snippet: However, not every covector field can be expressed this way. Those that can are referred to as exact differentials

annotations.note: Exact differentials/1-forms are not formalized on manifolds in Mathlib (no cotangent bundle or differential-form machinery present).

annotations.label: Tensor bundle

anchor.snippet: The tensor bundle is the direct sum of all tensor products of the tangent bundle and the cotangent bundle

annotations.note: There is no `TensorBundle` over a manifold in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Frame and frame bundle

anchor.snippet: A frame (or, in more precise terms, a tangent frame), is an ordered basis of particular tangent space

annotations.note: No frame bundle of a tangent bundle / principal GL_n-bundle is defined for manifolds in Mathlib.

annotations.label: k-th order tangent bundle and jet bundles

anchor.snippet: the k -th order tangent bundle is the collection of curves modulo the relation of k -th order contact

annotations.note: Higher-order tangent bundles and jet bundles are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: k-th order frame bundle

anchor.snippet: Define a k -th order frame to be the k -jet of a diffeomorphism from

annotations.note: k-th order frame bundles are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Differential / tangent map between manifolds

anchor.snippet: then the differential of f is a mapping df : T M → T N . It is also denoted by Tf and called the tangent map

annotations.note: `tangentMap I I' f : TangentBundle I M → TangentBundle I' M'` is exactly the tangent map (denoted `df` or `Tf`).

anchors.snippet: then the differential of f is a mapping df : T M → T N . It is also denoted by Tf and called the tangent map

annotations.label: Immersion, embedding and submersion

anchor.snippet: If m ≤ n , and f : M → N has rank m at p ∈ M , then f is called an immersion at p

annotations.note: `IsImmersion`/`IsImmersionAt` are formalized in `Manifold.Immersion`; submersions and smooth embeddings (beyond `Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.SmoothEmbedding`) are not fully present as named definitions.

annotations.label: Implicit function theorem for submersions

anchor.snippet: The implicit function theorem states that if f is a submersion at p , then M is locally a product of N and

annotations.note: The implicit function theorem exists for Banach spaces in `Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.Implicit`, but is not stated for submersions between manifolds.

anchors.snippet: The implicit function theorem states that if f is a submersion at p , then M is locally a product of N and

annotations.label: Lie derivative

anchor.snippet: A Lie derivative , named after Sophus Lie , is a derivation on the algebra of tensor fields over a manifold

annotations.note: The Lie bracket of vector fields (`mlieBracket`) is formalized; the general Lie derivative on tensor fields is not, since tensor fields themselves are not formalized.

annotations.label: Differential forms and degree

anchor.snippet: The bundle of differential forms , at each point, consists of all totally antisymmetric multilinear maps on the tangent space at that point

annotations.note: Differential forms on manifolds are not formalized in Mathlib (only the algebraic exterior/alternating algebra exists in linear algebra).

annotations.label: Exterior derivative

anchor.snippet: The exterior derivative is a linear operator on the graded vector space of all smooth differential forms on a smooth manifold

annotations.note: No exterior derivative `d` on manifold-valued differential forms is formalized.

annotations.label: Exterior derivative squared is zero

anchor.snippet: The exterior derivative also satisfies the identity

annotations.note: Without an exterior derivative on differential forms, `d ∘ d = 0` is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Closed and exact forms; de Rham cohomology

anchor.snippet: A form

annotations.note: De Rham cohomology and closed/exact differential forms are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equivalence of smooth atlases via diffeomorphism

anchor.snippet: one can define an equivalence relation on the space of smooth atlases on

annotations.note: `Diffeomorph` is defined and would underlie this equivalence relation, but the equivalence relation on smooth structures themselves is not given a name in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Smooth structures in low dimensions

anchor.snippet: If the dimension of

annotations.note: The theorem that every topological manifold of dimension ≤ 3 admits a unique smooth structure is not formalized.

annotations.label: Existence of topological manifolds with no smooth structure

anchor.snippet: Some topological manifolds admit no smooth structures, as was originally shown with a ten-dimensional example by Kervaire

annotations.note: Kervaire's example and the general existence of topological manifolds without smooth structure are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Exotic 7-spheres (Milnor)

anchor.snippet: Some topological manifolds admit many smooth structures which are not equivalent in the sense given above. This was originally discovered by John Milnor in the form of the exotic 7-spheres

annotations.note: The existence of an exotic 7-sphere is stated as a `proof_wanted` in `Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.PoincareConjecture`; the proof is not formalized.

annotations.label: Classification of 1-manifolds

anchor.snippet: Every one-dimensional connected smooth manifold is diffeomorphic to either

annotations.note: The classification of connected smooth 1-manifolds (diffeomorphic to ℝ or S^1) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Classification of compact 2-manifolds

anchor.snippet: every two-dimensional connected compact smooth manifold is diffeomorphic to one of the following

annotations.note: The classification of closed surfaces is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geometrization conjecture

anchor.snippet: the geometrization conjecture , stating loosely that any compact smooth 3-manifold can be split up into different parts, each of which admits Riemannian metrics which possess many symmetries

annotations.note: Thurston's geometrization conjecture is not stated or formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Undecidability of homeomorphism for 4-manifolds

anchor.snippet: the homeomorphism problem for 4-manifolds is undecidable

annotations.note: Undecidability of the homeomorphism problem for 4-manifolds is not formalized.

annotations.label: Freedman classification of simply connected 4-manifolds

anchor.snippet: Simply connected 4-manifolds have been classified up to homeomorphism by Freedman using the intersection form and Kirby–Siebenmann invariant

annotations.note: Freedman's classification of simply connected topological 4-manifolds is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Barden classification of simply connected 5-manifolds

anchor.snippet: This has been carried out to provide an explicit classification of simply connected 5-manifolds by Dennis Barden

annotations.note: Barden's classification of simply connected 5-manifolds is not formalized.

annotations.label: Riemannian manifold

anchor.snippet: A Riemannian manifold consists of a smooth manifold together with a positive-definite inner product on each of the individual tangent spaces

annotations.note: `IsRiemannianManifold I M` is the Prop-valued typeclass for a Riemannian metric on a smooth manifold, alongside `ContMDiffRiemannianMetric` for the metric data.

annotations.label: Pseudo-Riemannian manifold

anchor.snippet: A pseudo-Riemannian manifold (also called a semi-Riemannian manifold) is a generalization of the notion of Riemannian manifold where the inner products are allowed to have an indefinite signature

annotations.note: Only positive-definite Riemannian metrics are formalized; pseudo-Riemannian/Lorentzian metrics with indefinite signature are not.

annotations.label: Finsler manifold

anchor.snippet: A Finsler manifold is a different generalization of a Riemannian manifold, in which the inner product is replaced with a vector norm

annotations.note: Finsler manifolds are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symplectic manifold

anchor.snippet: A symplectic manifold is a manifold equipped with a closed , nondegenerate 2-form

annotations.note: Symplectic manifolds are not formalized in Mathlib (requires differential forms, which are absent on manifolds).

annotations.label: Symplectic manifolds are even-dimensional

anchor.snippet: This condition forces symplectic manifolds to be even-dimensional, due to the fact that skew-symmetric

annotations.note: Without symplectic manifolds, this proposition is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lie group

anchor.snippet: A Lie group consists of a

annotations.note: `LieGroup I n G` is defined as a group with a manifold structure such that multiplication and inversion are C^n.

annotations.label: Lie groups are parallelizable

anchor.snippet: The same argument shows, more generally, that every Lie group must be parallelizable

annotations.note: Parallelizability is not defined in Mathlib, and no theorem stating Lie groups are parallelizable was found.

annotations.label: Pseudogroup

anchor.snippet: A pseudogroup consists of a topological space S and a collection Γ consisting of homeomorphisms from open subsets of S to other open subsets of S such that

annotations.note: `StructureGroupoid H` formalizes the notion of pseudogroup (called a structure groupoid in Mathlib) on a topological space H.

annotations.label: Atlas compatible with a pseudogroup

anchor.snippet: is said to be compatible with a pseudogroup Γ provided that the transition functions

annotations.note: `HasGroupoid M G` says the charts of M have transition maps in the structure groupoid G, exactly the notion of pseudogroup-compatible atlas.

annotations.label: Manifolds as pseudogroup atlases

anchor.snippet: A differentiable manifold is then an atlas compatible with the pseudogroup of

annotations.note: `IsManifold I n M` extends `HasGroupoid M (contDiffGroupoid n I)`, presenting a C^n manifold as a charted space compatible with the C^n pseudogroup.

annotations.label: Structure sheaf giving a C^k manifold

anchor.snippet: A structure sheaf C k is said to give M the structure of a C k manifold of dimension n provided that, for any p ∈ M , there exists a neighborhood U of p

annotations.note: The smooth structure sheaf is built in `Sheaf.Smooth` and `ChartedSpace.locallyRingedSpace`, but Mathlib does not define a manifold by abstractly specifying a structure sheaf with local-model condition.

annotations.label: Differentiable manifold via locally ringed space

anchor.snippet: A differentiable manifold (of class C k ) consists of a pair ( M , O M ) where M is a second countable Hausdorff space , and O M is a sheaf of local

annotations.note: Mathlib produces the locally ringed space from a smooth manifold (`ChartedSpace.locallyRingedSpace`) but does not adopt the locally-ringed-space definition as the primary definition of a manifold.

annotations.label: Cotangent space via maximal ideal

anchor.snippet: The cotangent space at a point is I p / I p 2 , where I p is the maximal ideal of the stalk

annotations.note: The cotangent space `m_p/m_p^2` of a smooth manifold's structure sheaf is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Algebra of scalars on a manifold

anchor.snippet: the set of real-valued C k functions on the manifold forms an algebra under pointwise addition and multiplication, called the algebra of scalar fields or simply the algebra of scalars

annotations.note: `C^n⟮I, M; 𝓘(ℝ), ℝ⟯` (i.e. `ContMDiffMap` to ℝ) carries a commutative ring/algebra structure (see `Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.Algebra.SmoothFunctions`).

annotations.label: Reconstruction of M from algebra of scalars (Banach–Stone)

anchor.snippet: It is possible to reconstruct a manifold from its algebra of scalars, first as a set, but also as a topological space – this is an application of the Banach–Stone theorem

annotations.note: Neither the Banach–Stone theorem nor reconstruction of a manifold from its algebra of smooth functions is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Points of M correspond to algebra homomorphisms

anchor.snippet: there is a one-to-one correspondence between the points of M and the algebra homomorphisms

annotations.note: The correspondence between points of M and ℝ-algebra homomorphisms `C^∞(M) → ℝ` is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tangent bundle via derivations

anchor.snippet: the tangent bundle to M can be defined as the derivations of the algebra of smooth functions on M

annotations.note: `Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.DerivationBundle` constructs pointwise derivations of the smooth function algebra and relates them to the tangent space, but the identification of the tangent bundle with global derivations is not stated as a theorem.
